---
title: "scRNASeq Analyze"
author: "O. Fari"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    df_print: paged
    keep_md: true
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '4'
  rmdformats::robobook:
    self_contained: true
    thumbnails: true
    lightbox: true
    gallery: true
    highlight: tango
  html_notebook:
    theme: spacelab
    highlight: textmate
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    df_print: paged
    toc_float: true
---


***
# Load libraries
```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = TRUE)
# load required libraries
library(knitr)
library("tidyverse")
library("conflicted")
library("BiocParallel")
library("ggplot2")
library("readr")
library("RColorBrewer")
library("dplyr")
conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("count", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("rowRanges", "MatrixGenerics")
library("ggpubr")
library("tibble")
conflict_prefer("paste", "base")
conflict_prefer("rename", "dplyr")
library("tidyr")
library("org.Hs.eg.db") 
library("org.Mm.eg.db") 
conflict_prefer("desc", "dplyr")

library(Seurat)
library(Matrix)
library(RCurl)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(AnnotationHub)
library(ensembldb)
library(multtest)
library(glmGamPoi)
library(metap)
library(patchwork)

library(sctransform)
library(SingleR)
library(MAST)
library(DESeq2)
library(Nebulosa)
library(celldex)
library(ggrepel)
library("ComplexHeatmap")
library("circlize")

save_plot <- function(filename, p, width=NULL, height=NULL) {
  if (!is.null(width) && !is.null(height)) {
   # ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".png")), plot = p, width = width, height = height)
   # ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".svg")), plot = p, width = width, height = height)
    ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = width, height = height)
    
  } else {
   # ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".png")), plot = p)
  #  ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".svg")), plot = p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotsdir, paste0("/",filename, ".pdf")), plot = p)
  }
}
save_RDS <- function(dir, object,filename){
  saveRDS(object, file.path(
    dir,paste0(prefix,"/","_",filename,".RDS")))
}

```


# Single Cell Main Analysis from: https://satijalab.org/seurat/articles/pbmc3k_tutorial.html
```{r load matrix data, include = FALSE}
# load matrix and create seurat object - Didn't really use it in the end
expression_matrix <- ReadMtx(
  mtx = "/srv/workspace/rprojects/USER/scRNA_analysis_Renia/data/matrix.mtx.gz", features = "/srv/workspace/rprojects/USER/scRNA_analysis_Renia/data/features.tsv.gz",
  cells = "/srv/workspace/rprojects/USER/scRNA_analysis_Renia/data/barcodes.tsv.gz"
)
seurat_object <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = expression_matrix)


```


# With pagoda filtering steps: https://pklab.med.harvard.edu/pagoda2.walkthrough.html
# https://satijalab.org/seurat/archive/v4.3/integration_introduction
```{r QC filtering and normalising}
# Read the matrix count of raw data -needs gz compression
counts_raw <- Read10X(data.dir =  "/srv/workspace/rprojects/USER/scRNA_analysis_Renia/data")

# First filtering genes any cells that don't fit the expected detected gene vs molecule count relationship (molecules per cell and molecules per gene - using Pagoda like Tatiana)
library(pagoda2)

count_filt <- gene.vs.molecule.cell.filter(
 counts_raw, max.cell.size = 5e4, min.cell.size = 500)

# Here we added the names.delim to use the suffix and create an orig. ident for the 4 samples
seurat_object <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = count_filt, names.delim = "-", names.field = 2)

head(seurat_object)

# I am trying to create a column with our samples names as character for later use
# Define sample mapping
sample_ref <- c(
  "1" = "wtp3428",
  "2" = "wtn3429",
  "3" = "kop3430",
  "4" = "kon3431"
)

# Careful with factor vs character values and name barcode mapping -wasn't recognising barcode
converted_ids <- sample_ref[as.character(seurat_object$orig.ident)]
names(converted_ids) <- colnames(seurat_object)  # ✅ this is the key fix!
seurat_object$sample_id <- converted_ids        
head(seurat_object)

seurat_object$genotype <- substr(seurat_object$sample_id, 1, 2)


#Add column with mitochondrial gene percentage
seurat_object[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(seurat_object, pattern = "^mt-")

head(seurat_object@meta.data, 50)

VlnPlot(seurat_object, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

#Filter mt percent
seurat_object_filt_mt <- subset(seurat_object, percent.mt < 20)

VlnPlot(seurat_object_filt_mt, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)


# Split object and integrate data
# Split the object into a list of sample-specific objects (wt and ko)
seurat_list <- SplitObject(object = seurat_object_filt_mt, split.by = "sample_id") #genotype 22 clusters, sample_id   
head(seurat_object_filt_mt)

# Normalize and identify variable features for each dataset independently
seurat_list <- lapply(X = seurat_list, FUN = function(x) {
    x <- NormalizeData(x)
    x <- FindVariableFeatures(x, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)
 })

# select features that are repeatedly variable across datasets for integration
features <- SelectIntegrationFeatures(object.list = seurat_list)

data.anchors <- FindIntegrationAnchors(object.list = seurat_list, anchor.features = features)

# this command creates an 'integrated' data assay
data.combined <- IntegrateData(anchorset = data.anchors)


# A list of cell cycle markers, from Tirosh et al, 2015, is loaded with Seurat.  We can segregate this list into markers of G2/M phase and markers of S phase 
# Homology of human genes to mouse genes

library(gprofiler2)
mmus_s = gorth(cc.genes.updated.2019$s.genes, source_organism = "hsapiens", target_organism = "mmusculus")$ortholog_name
mmus_g2m = gorth(cc.genes.updated.2019$g2m.genes, source_organism = "hsapiens", target_organism = "mmusculus")$ortholog_name


#Scale data and regressing cell cycle out- previous versions used to be with RegressOut (Tatiana)

data.combined<- CellCycleScoring(
  data.combined, s.features = mmus_s, g2m.features = mmus_g2m, set.ident = TRUE)
  
head(data.combined)


# specify that we will perform downstream analysis on the corrected data note that the
# original unmodified data still resides in the 'RNA' assay
DefaultAssay(data.combined) <- "integrated"


saveRDS(data.combined, file = "seurat_object_integr_regr.RDS")

#Import the saved the regressed object, takes too long to run!

#seurat_object_regr <- readRDS(


#Run PCA
data.combined<- ScaleData(data.combined, vars.to.regress = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score"))

data.combined <- RunPCA(data.combined, features = VariableFeatures(object = data.combined))

DimHeatmap(data.combined, dims = 1, cells = 500, balanced = TRUE)

ElbowPlot(data.combined)

#Cluster and UMAP- not sure how many dimensions I should choose- go with more better than less
data.combined <- RunUMAP(data.combined, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
data.combined <- FindNeighbors(data.combined, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
data.combined <- FindClusters(data.combined, resolution = 0.5)

p <- DimPlot(data.combined, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)
p

save_plot(filename = "Umap_all_clusters", p = p)


# Plot top markers for all clusters
p1 <- DotPlot(data.combined, features = c("S100a8", "Retnlg", "Cd79a","Ighm", "Spp1", "Thbs1", "Fcgr1", "Ki67", "Epcam", "Lgals4", "Krt19",  "C1qc", "Apoe", "Cadps", "Lars2", "Gzma", "Trbc2", "Cd8a", "Nkg7", "Cd4", "Lef1", "Foxp3", "Ctla4", "Xcr1", "Cd209a", "Jchain", "Igkc", "Il17a", "Ccr7", "Hmox1", "Mcpt1", "Gata2", "Siglech",  "Flt3", "Col1a1")) + coord_flip()
p1
save_plot(filename = "Markers_clusters", p = p1, width = 12, height = 7)


#Exporting all markers in excel
library(openxlsx)

#Find all markers with % of cells and logfc tresholds- fitting to Tatiana's excel -Wilcoxon rank sum
cluster_markers <- FindAllMarkers(
 object = data.combined, 
  only.pos = TRUE,               # only positive markers
 # min.pct = 0.1,          # expressed in at least 10% of cells in either group-used for diffexp test
  logfc.threshold = 0.25         # minimum log2 fold change
)


# Filter by adjusted p-value & optional logFC-fits with Tatiana's excel
filtered_markers <- cluster_markers %>%
 filter(p_val_adj < 0.05, abs(avg_log2FC) >= 0.25) 
filtered_markers <- filtered_markers %>%
  select(gene, everything())


# Select top 10 markers per cluster based on avg_log2FC
top_markers_logFC <- filtered_markers %>%
 group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_log2FC, n = 20)

head(top_markers_logFC)

#Export to Excel with multiple sheets

# Create workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Add logFC-based markers as one sheet
addWorksheet(wb, "Top_by_logFC")
writeData(wb, "Top_by_logFC", top_markers_logFC)


# Also (optional): Export full filtered markers as extra sheet
addWorksheet(wb, "Filtered_all")
writeData(wb, "Filtered_all", filtered_markers)

# Save Excel file
saveWorkbook(wb, "Seurat_Markers_Output_Integr_samplesplit.xlsx")
sessionInfo()
```


```{r subclustering}

myeloid_subcl <- subset(data.combined, idents = c("0", "1", "4", "8", "12", "13", "15"))

myeloid_subcl <- FindVariableFeatures(myeloid_subcl, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)

myeloid_subcl<- ScaleData(myeloid_subcl, vars.to.regress = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score"))

myeloid_subcl <- RunPCA(myeloid_subcl, features = VariableFeatures(object = myeloid_subcl))

DimHeatmap(myeloid_subcl, dims = 1, cells = 500, balanced = TRUE)

ElbowPlot(myeloid_subcl)

myeloid_subcl <- RunUMAP(myeloid_subcl, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
myeloid_subcl <- FindNeighbors(myeloid_subcl, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
myeloid_subcl <- FindClusters(myeloid_subcl, resolution = 0.5)

p <- DimPlot(myeloid_subcl, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)
p

cluster_markers <- FindAllMarkers(
 object = myeloid_subcl, 
  only.pos = TRUE,               # only positive markers
 # min.pct = 0.1,          # expressed in at least 10% of cells in either group-used for diffexp test
  logfc.threshold = 0.25         # minimum log2 fold change
)
```

# Automated cluster annotation using SingleR from AI
```{r automated annotation,include=FALSE}

# Load libraries
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(SummarizedExperiment)

# Load your single-cell dataset
# Example: Load a Seurat object
seurat_object_autom <- data.combined

# Convert Seurat object to SingleCellExperiment
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seurat_object_autom)

# Load mouse reference dataset
ref <- celldex::MouseRNAseqData()

# Run SingleR annotation
pred <- SingleR(test = sce, ref = ref, labels = ref$label.main)

# Attach annotations back to your Seurat object
seurat_object_autom$SingleR_labels <- pred$labels

# Optionally visualize the annotation
Seurat::DimPlot(seurat_object_autom, group.by = "SingleR_labels", reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE)




```


# Load-prepare dataset & add annotation
```{r load data, include=FALSE}
load("/srv/workspace/rprojects/USER/Seurat_Myeloid/data/seurat_object_myl.Rdata")

annotation <- data.frame(seurat_clusters = c(0:11),
                         Celltype = c("Macro_C1qc", "Granulo_S100a9",
                                      "Macro_Vegfa", "Granulo_Cxcl2","cDC1_CD209a", "Monos_chil3", 
                                      "DCs_act", "Granulo_Homox1","pDCs", "Mast","Basophilic", "Granulo_Mmp8"))
annotation$seurat_clusters <- as.factor(annotation$seurat_clusters)
metadata <- seurat_object_myl@meta.data %>%
  add_column(Genotype = substr(rownames(seurat_object_myl@meta.data),1,3)) %>%
  add_column(Genotype_short = substr(rownames(seurat_object_myl@meta.data),1,2)) %>%
  add_column(rowIDs = rownames(seurat_object_myl@meta.data))
head(seurat_object_myl)

#metadata$CellGeno <- (paste(metadata$Celltype, "_", metadata$Genotype_short))
  

meta_merge <- left_join(metadata, annotation) %>%
  column_to_rownames("rowIDs") 
meta_merge$CellGeno <- (paste0(meta_merge$Celltype, "_", meta_merge$Genotype_short))




all(seurat_object_myl@meta.data$nCount_RNA == meta_merge$nCount_RNA)
all(rownames(meta_merge) == rownames(seurat_object_myl@meta.data))


seurat_object_myl@meta.data <- meta_merge
# ### workaround color dimplot error: Fehler: Must request at least one colour from a hue palette.
# seurat_object_myl$cell_label <- Idents(seurat_object_myl)
  
  

```
